tweets.text:
The hook formula:

[Unexpected fact] + [Simple explanation] + [Relatable takeaway]

Example:
'Teens share 3x more. Not addiction. They believe their voice matters.'

Make people feel smart for reading.

African fintech revolution:

• M-Pesa: 50M+ users
• Flutterwave: $2B+ processed
• Chipper Cash: 5M users

All built because traditional banking failed the masses.
Necessity breeds innovation.

CBDC design choices:

• Account vs Token-based
• Direct vs Intermediated
• Interest-bearing vs Not
• Programmable vs Neutral

Each choice has tradeoffs. No perfect design.

DeFi composability:

• Money legos: Protocols plug into each other
• Example: Deposit → Lend → Borrow → Farm
• Risk: Cascade failures
• Reward: Innovation velocity

Open systems move faster.

Why Africa will lead Web3 adoption:

1. Young, mobile-first population
2. Weak legacy banking
3. Currency instability
4. Remittance dependency
5. Entrepreneurial culture

Leapfrogging Web2 entirely.

Crypto vs Sanctions:

• Russia: Using crypto to bypass restrictions
• North Korea: $1B+ stolen via hacks
• Iran: Mining as revenue source

Regulators caught between innovation and control.

FOMO design principles:

1. Scarcity (limited spots/features)
2. Social proof (X people joined)
3. Time pressure (24hr window)
4. Exclusivity (invite-only)

Gas used all 4. Perfect execution.

Stablecoin use cases beyond trading:

• Payroll (global teams)
• Savings (inflation hedging)
• Payments (B2B, P2P)
• Credit (collateral)
• Remittances (obvious one)

Real utility exists. It's just quieter than speculation.

The difference between good and great products:

Good products solve problems.
Great products create feelings.

TBH didn't solve a problem.
It created moments of joy.

Joy > Utility for virality.

UAE digital dirham:

• Strategy: Crypto hub positioning
• Approach: Crypto-friendly regulation
• CBDC: Wholesale focus first
• Goal: Bridge TradFi and crypto

Regulatory arbitrage as strategy.
